cv_text:
## Ahmed Saad

<LOCATION>, <LOCATION>

## <EMAIL>

PhD Candidate in Materials Engineering with expertise in nanomaterials, diagnostics, biomaterials, surface modifications, and advanced material characterization. Experienced in interdisciplinary collaboration and eager to drive innovation in medical devices and diagnostics.

## EDUCATION

PhD Materials Engineering , McGill University -Montreal, QC 2021 -April 2026 (expected)

- Cumulative GPA: 3.7 / 4.00
- I am working under supervision of Prof. <CANDIDATE_NAME> on surface modification of polymeric materials for biomedical applications.

## MSc. Materials Engineering , McGill University -Montreal, QC 2018 - 2020

- Cumulative GPA: 3.8 / 4.00
- I worked on implant surface modifications for tissue attachment.

## Bachelor of Nanoscience, <LOCATION> Science and Technology -Cairo, <LOCATION> 2014 - 2018

- Cumulative GPA: 3.9 / 4.00, graduated with honors <CANDIDATE_NAME>
- Significant knowledge of nanomaterials and polymers.

## PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE

## Material scientist, Noze -Montreal, QC Jan 2025 - May 2025

- I developed chemo-resistive sensors using printable composite nanomaterials for the detection of Volatile Organic Compounds in breath.

## Trainee, Lab2Market Validate -Montreal, QC Jan 2024 - May 2024

- I Completed an intensive program on market research , customer discovery, and business model validation to test the commercial potential of implant surface engineering technologies.
- I Conducted over 50 customer interviews and industry consultations to validate productmarket fit for research-based innovations.

## Teaching assistant and grading, McGill University -Montreal, QC 2022 - 2024

- I worked as a teaching assistant and a grader of multiple courses in Materials Engineering.

## Research Intern, Nabta Health -UAE June 2019 - Sep 2019

- I worked under the CSO to develop a Point-Of-Care medical device.

## SKILLS

## Technical :

- Testing standards (ASTM, ISO 13485)
- Polymer synthesis and surface modifications
- Nanomaterial fabrication and characterization
- Advanced characterization techniques
- o UV -Vis spectroscopy
- o Scanning electron microscopy (SEM)
- o Optical microscopy
- o Atomic force microscopy (AFM)
- o FTIR spectroscopy
- o X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS)
- Experience with Python and MATLAB
- Experience with SolidWorks and ImageJ

## Soft skills:

- Communication and Organizational Skills
- Detail-Oriented and Precision with Instruments
- Safety Protocol Adherence
- Team Collaboration

## SELECTED PUBLICATIONS

- Saad A , <CANDIDATE_NAME>, Tran SD, Tamimi F, Aparicio C, <CANDIDATE_NAME> "Controlling collagen I orientation on polyetheretherketone implants to improve epithelial sealing." ACS Biomaterials Science & Engineering. (2025).
- Atsu, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, A ., et al. "Diazonium chemistry for bonding between PEEK and resin cement." The Journal of Prosthetic Dentistry (2025).
- Saad, A. , et al. "Biomimetic Strategy to Enhance Epithelial Cell Viability and Spreading on PEEK Implants." ACS Biomaterials Science & Engineering (2022).
- Saad, A. , and <LOCATION>, M. "Diazonium Salts and Related Compounds for Biomedical Applications." Aryl Diazonium Salts and Related Compounds. <LOCATION>, 2022.
- Moussa, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, A ., et al. ' High toughness resorbable brushite-gypsum fiber-reinforced cements. ' Materials Science and Engineering: C, (2021).

## CONFERENCE PARTICIPATION

- Canadian Biomaterial Society Conference (2025) -Poster
- Canadian Biomaterial Society Conference (2023) -Talk
- McGill Regenerative Medicine Symposium (2023) -Poster
- Canadian Biomaterial Society Conference (2022) -Poster
- Quebec Centre for Advanced Materials Symposium (2019) -Poster

## AWARDS

- Mitacs Globallink Research Award | 2024
- Mitacs Business Strategy Internship Program | 2024
- Fonds de recherche du Québec (FRQNT) Doctoral Scholarship | 2023 - 2025
- McGill Engineering Graduate Award | 2021 - 2024
- Graduate Excellence Fellowship Award, McGill University | 2018 - 2024

## VOLUNTEERING AND OUTREACH

- Canadian Biomaterials Society (CBS) student board representative | 2023-2024
- Mentoring in Summer Undergraduate Research in Engineering (SURE) at McGill University |2021-2023
- McGill Institute for Advanced Materials (MIAM) trainee committee member |2021-2023

## Languages

- English: Proficient

- French: Intermediate
pitch_text:
Selected Job Offer Position: R&D Scientist, Structural Heart & Aortic (Medtronic Hypothetical) Key Requirements:

- PhD in Materials Science, Biomedical Engineering, or related.
- Experience with polymer characterization and surface modification.
- Knowledge of tissue-material interactions and biocompatibility.

## The Elevator Pitch

"Hi, I'm <CANDIDATE_NAME>. I am a PhD candidate specializing in the one thing that determines if an implant succeeds or fails: The Interface.

In my research, I focused on modifying the surface of PEEK implants to make them actively bond with bone tissue, rather than just sitting there. I've spent years mastering the exact skills listed in your job description: surface chemistry, cell culture, and characterizing how new materials interact with the human body.

I see that Medtronic is pushing the boundaries of what structural heart devices can do. You need materials that last longer and integrate better. Because I have a deep background in both the chemistry of the materials and the biology of the host response, I can help your R&D team solve biocompatibility issues before they become expensive failures in clinical trials.

I'm looking to move from academic discovery to industrial application, and I'd love to bring my expertise in bioactive surfaces to your team."

<LOCATION>

clarify_text.ikigai:
My Ikigai is translating advanced material science into restorative patient solutions .

The central theme of my career is "Integration." My research-improving how synthetic implants (like PEEK) integrate with living bone-reflects my broader professional desire: to bridge the gap between academic discovery and clinical application . My talent lies in understanding the complex interface where biology meets engineering (surface chemistry, cell adhesion), a niche that the medical device industry (Medtronic, <CANDIDATE_NAME>) needs to master to create the next generation of implants.

I can create a lasting impact by ensuring that medical devices are not just "biocompatible" (safe) but "bioactive" (healing). To align my career with this, I am ready to transition from the "publish or perish" mindset of academia to the "patient-first" and "regulatory-compliant" mindset of industry. I am willing to trade the freedom of blue-sky research for the rigor of developing marketable, FDA-approved products.
clarify_text.ideal_day:
My ideal day balances scientific rigor with strategic application .

It begins with reviewing technical data-perhaps analyzing surface characterization results (XPS or SEM) for a new implant coating. I enjoy being the "expert in the room" whom engineers consult when they encounter a biological failure.

By mid-day, I shift to a cross-functional role. I am in a meeting with the Regulatory Affairs team, explaining why a specific chemical modification is safe and effective, helping them draft the submission for a new device. I enjoy translating complex science into clear, defensible arguments for stakeholders. The day ends with a sense of progress-knowing that the science I worked on today isn't just for a paper, but for a product that will eventually go into a patient.
clarify_text.smart_goals:
- Goal 1 (Career Transition): Secure an R&D or Medical Affairs position at a top-tier medical device company (Medtronic, <CANDIDATE_NAME>, or similar) by September 2026.
- Measurable: Submit 3 high-quality applications per week; secure 2 informational interviews with industry scientists per month.
- Attainable: Leveraging my permanent residency status and current network.
- Goal 2 (Academic Closure): Successfully defend my PhD thesis and submit one final first-author paper on surface modification of PEEK implants by May 2026.
- Measurable: Thesis submitted to committee; manuscript submitted to Biomaterials journal.
- Realistic: Data collection is complete; currently in the writing phase.
- Goal 3 (Skill Acquisition): Complete a certificate or short course in "Regulatory Affairs for Medical Devices" (focus on FDA/Health Canada pathways) by July 2026.
- Measurable: Certificate earned.
- Time-bound: 6 months. This bridges my technical gap for my 3rd preferred job choice.

preferences.target_companies: I am open to any company but some of the companies that interest me are Medtronic and Johnson & Johnson.
